reachability_artifacts: go test ./internal/telemetry/... -v — TestFireSkipsEmptyCmd PASS, TestFireStillFiresRealCmd PASS, TestFireTelemetryMetaCommandExcluded PASS

design_decisions.choice: Mirror the existing meta-command exclusion shape: add `cmd == ""` early-return immediately after `cmd == "telemetry"` inside Fire().
options: Add check after Fire() goroutine spawn
Add check at call site in cmd/sworn/main.go
Mirror existing early-return pattern inside Fire()
design_decisions.rationale: The existing `cmd == "telemetry"` early-return (line 205-207 of telemetry.go) is the pattern: check, return before spawning the goroutine. Adding `cmd == ""` immediately after is the obvious, minimal extension. Consistency with the existing codebase and zero new patterns.

design_decisions.choice: Empty string is the precise no-args/TUI signal — no separate flag or parameter.
options: Add a separate isTUI parameter to Fire()
design_decisions.rationale: Every real subcommand has a name resolved by the command registry. An empty `cmd` means `os.Args` had ≤1 element — exactly the TUI launch path in main.go's dispatch(). No risk of false positives.

design_decisions.choice: Hard-coded exclusion — no new exported API, config toggle, or environment variable.
options: Configurable via env var or flag
design_decisions.rationale: The spec says 'extend Fire()' — this is a hard-coded exclusion, not a configurable policy. Adding a toggle would be over-engineering for a slice this narrow.

design_decisions.choice: Check-and-return happens synchronously inside Fire(), before the goroutine spawn.
options: Check inside Fire() before goroutine spawn
Signal goroutine to cancel via context
Check in main.go before calling Fire()
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the existing `cmd == "telemetry"` check exactly — the exclusion is decided before the goroutine spawn. No race window, no goroutine to cancel.

design_decisions.choice: Two-test strategy: one negative (empty cmd → no-op) + one positive (real cmd still fires).
options: One negative + one positive guard test
Single table-driven test with all cases
design_decisions.rationale: TestFireSkipsEmptyCmd asserts the transport is never called for cmd="". TestFireStillFiresRealCmd asserts Fire("verify", ...) still hits the transport — this guards against an over-broad exclusion. The existing TestFireTelemetryMetaCommandExcluded covers the meta-command case.
